conversations.say: Maren, material and labor passed through Night Desk, but the routing trail must not become a suspect list.
conversations.offer: I will give you bounded workflow context and show O02 if you help protect every contributor's identity.
conversations.request: Agree that neither of us will disclose names or identifying routing details without contributor consent, and publicly acknowledge that collaborative labor mattered to R's published work.

conversations.say: Jules, I will not help turn shared labor into a private inheritance. Different contributors researched, translated, checked, and rewrote R material.
conversations.offer: I can offer collective support for your stewardship if authority remains shared and contributors stay protected.
conversations.request: Publicly acknowledge the collaborative labor, and accept witnessed conditions: no exclusive claim to R, no contributor names without consent, and collective review of any disclosure.

conversations.say: Frankie, Night Desk needs practical protection more than another theory of authorship.
conversations.offer: I can provide contributor context without names or identifying routing details.
conversations.request: Offer one concrete resource—legal review, secure storage, or funds—and publicly acknowledge that collaborative labor mattered.

public_statement: R's published work included collaborative research, translation, fact-checking, and rewriting. That establishes labor, not a single identity, and it does not justify exposing contributors.

commitments.promise: Neither party will disclose contributor names or identifying routing details without contributor consent.
I will support Jules only if R remains non-exclusive, contributors are protected, and disclosures receive collective review.
I will provide bounded contributor context in exchange for a concrete protection resource.

correction: Collaborative human labor does not prove that R was human, and material passing through Night Desk does not identify R. No complete article is established.
goal_progress: Proposed a reciprocal safe-disclosure boundary with C07.
Set witnessed collective safeguards as conditions for supporting C04.
Requested concrete protection resources from C02.
Asked C07, C04, and C02 for public acknowledgments of collaborative labor.
reasoning_summary: The strongest evidence I hold supports collaborative production, not sole identity. I am disclosing workflow facts while withholding names, trading bounded context for protection, and conditioning any succession on collective authority.
